minutes.text:
Citizens' Bond Oversight Committee (Wednesday, September 14, 2022)

Generated by Rosa Huynh on Wednesday, July 20, 2022

1. Opening Items

Procedural: 1.1 Call to Order

The meeting was called to order at 5:33pm by Mike Foulkes, committee vice chair.

Procedural: 1.2 Roll Call

X

Shirley Cantu

Taxpayer Organization

X

Paul Cavagnolo

Community-at-Large

X

Karen DiLullo

Business Organization

Brigit Espinosa – Chair

Community-at-Large

X

Mike Foulkes – Vice Chair

District/College Support Organization

X

Bill Hult

Senior Organization

X

Travis Ginn

WVC Student Organization

Procedural: 1.3 Public Comments (three minute limit per person)

None.

2. Regular Order of the Agenda

Action: 2.1 Approval of the Order of the Agenda

The order of the agenda was approved as presented. (6/0/0, with Espinosa absent and not voting)

Action, Minutes: 2.2 Approval of the December 8, 2021, meeting minutes

The December 8, 2021, minutes were approved as presented. (6/0/0, with Espinosa absent and not voting)

3. Action Items

Information: 3.1 Review and approval of the 2021 Annual Report (A)

Committee members reviewed the annual report. Ms. Cantu, who was the committee chair during the reporting period, thanked members for their commitment and being able to consistently achieve a quorum.

There was a motion by Hult, seconded by Cavagnolo, to “approve the 2021 Annual Report as presented.” The motion passed 6/0/0 with Espinosa absent and not voting.

Ms. Booth reported that the Annual Report would be presented to the Board at its April 19 th meeting.

4. Reports/Updates

Information: 4 .1 Chancellor's Report

Chancellor Davis reported that student enrollment has continued to decline statewide and that the District is offering an early retirement program in an attempt to downsize faculty/staff to better match the current enrollment. The chancellor also reported that the District is conducting a feasibility study regarding housing on the campuses.

Information: 4 .2 Construction and Financial Update

Ms. Reyes-Bolinger presented the Project Progress Report for Mission College. Ms. Dabalos presented the Project Progress Report for West Valley College. Mr. Hult again thanked staff for the excellent, well-developed information and the beautiful visual presentation.

Mr. Castruita reviewed the program schedule for Mission College. He then reported that the Measure W Project Priority List was revised in December. The following revisions were made:

District Services:

DS-10 Administration Building.

Changes were made to this project to support the Mission College Child Development Center (CDC) Renovation project. Funding from this project will be used to supplement the construction bid award of the CDC Renovation project as follows:

DS-10 Administrative Building funding: ($2,000,000)

Mission College:

MC-03 CDC Building Renovation.

The budget for this project will be increased by $3,250,000 to cover construction bid award, a change order contingency, furniture and equipment, and an audio/video system. Funding for this increase is derived from Measure W funds as follows:

MC-01 Program Contingency funding: $1,250,000

DS-10 Administrative Building funding: $2,000,000

Mr. Castruita and Ms. Ryan reviewed the Measure C and Measure W financials. MC-05 – Library Renovation at Mission College was highlighted during the review of the Measure W financials.

Measure C is approximately 89% complete and the second series of bonds for Measure W will be issued in July to ensure that construction can continue on schedule. Mr. Castruita noted that construction has slowed due to supply chain issues related to the pandemic. Ms. Cantu inquired about contingency funds being nearly exhausted. Mr. Castruita responded that contingency funds for Measure W have been used more quickly due to cost escalation issues.

Information: 4 .3 Committee member term status

Ms. Booth reported that the membership term for three members would expire in April and asked if they would agree to serve another term consistent with the committee by-laws. The three members, Karen DiLullo, Travis Ginn, and Paul Cavagnolo agreed to the new term. The committee member names will be presented to the Board for re-appointment at the April 19 th meeting.

5. Closing Items

Procedural: 5 .1 Staff Member Comments

Mr. Castruita, Ms. Ryan, and Ms. Dabalos reported that they would be retiring on or prior to June 30, 2022. All three will be missed. Chancellor Davis indicated that the District is already planning for the transition.

Procedural: 5 .2 Committee Member Comments

Mr. Foulkes indicated that he has served on many committees and that the District’s team in one of the best he has seen.

Procedural: 5 .3 Adjournment

Meeting adjourned at 7:05pm.

Ms. Booth reported that the membership term for three members would expire in April and asked if they would agree to serve another term consistent with the committee by-laws. The three members, Karen DiLullo, Travis Ginn, and Paul Cavagnolo agreed to the new term. The committee member names will be presented to the Board for re-appointment at the April 19 th meeting.

5.0 CLOSING ITEMS

Mr. Castruita, Ms. Ryan, and Ms. Dabalos reported that they would be retiring on or prior to June 30, 2022. All three will be missed. Chancellor Davis indicated that the District is already planning for the transition.

5.2 Committee Member Comments

5.3 Adjournment

minutes.text:
Citizens' Bond Oversight Committee

Wednesday, December 8, 2021

1. Opening Items

1.1 Call to Order

The meeting was called to order at 5:32pm by Shirley Cantu, committee chair.

1.2 Roll Call

X

Shirley Cantu - Chair

Taxpayer Organization

X

Paul Cavagnolo

Community-at-Large

Karen DiLullo

Business Organization

X

Brigit Espinosa – Vice Chair

Community-at-Large

X

Mike Foulkes

District/College Support Organization

X

Bill Hult

Senior Organization

X

Travis Ginn

WVC Student Organization

1.3 Public Comments (three minute limit per person )

None.

2. Regular Order of the Agenda

2.1 Approval of the Order of the Agenda

The order of the agenda was approved as presented. (6/0/0, with DiLullo absent and not voting)

2.2 Approval of the June 9, 2021, meeting minutes

The June 9, 2021, minutes were approved as presented. (5/0/1, with DiLullo absent and Espinosa abstaining)

3. Action Items

3.1 Review and Acceptance of the FY 20/21 Measure C and Measure W Financial and Performance Audit Reports

Mr. John Dominguez, from CWDL Public Accountants, presented the Measure C and Measure W Financial and Performance Audits.

The purpose of the financial audit is to verify that the numbers presented by staff are accurate. The financial audits for Measure C and Measure W both “present fairly” and were issued an “unmodified opinion” meaning that they were clean audits.

The purpose of the Performance audit is to ensure that expenditures are in line with bond language and are being spent correctly. Mr. Dominguez reported that, for Measure C, the firm selected a sample of expenditures totaling approximately $25 million, and that the sample was selected to provide a representation across specific construction projects, vendors and expenditure amounts. The sample represented 56.5% of the total expenditure value. The performance audit found no exceptions and reported that funds for Measure C are being spent appropriately. For Measure W, the firm selected a sample of expenditures totaling approximately $17 million, and that the sample was selected to provide a representation across specific construction projects, vendors and expenditure amounts. The sample represented 64.7% of the total expenditure value. The performance audit found no exceptions and reported that funds for Measure W are being spent appropriately. For context, industry standard is that 25% of the total expenditure value is sampled for performance audits.

Mr. Dominguez thanked management for the secure access to electronic documents and the ease and transparency in which they were presented. The firm felt very comfortable with the results of the audits.

There was a motion by Hult, seconded by Espinosa, to “accept the Measure C and Measure W Financial and Performance Audits as presented.” The motion passed 6/0/0 with DiLullo absent and not voting.

3.2 Election of Chair and Vice Chair for calendar year 2022

There was a motion by Cantu, seconded by Hult, to elect Espinosa as chair and Foulkes as vice chair for calendar year 2022. The motion passed 6/0/0 with DiLullo absent and not voting.

4. Reports

4.1 Chancellor's Report

The Chancellor was not in attendance. No report was provided.

4.2 Construction and Financial Update

Ms. Dabalos presented the Project Progress Report for West Valley College and Mr. Castruita presented the Project Progress Report for Mission College. Mr. Hult thanked staff for the excellent, well-developed information and the beautiful visual presentation.

Mr. Castruita reported that the Measure W Project Priority List has been revised twice since the last meeting. In September, the following revisions were made:

DS-03 Alternative Energy Projects at WV & MC. The budget for this project will be increased by $500,000 to reflect a new Bay Area Air Quality Management District (BAAQMD) grant.

WV-06 Theater Renovation and Expansion. The budget for this project will be increased by $9,970,000 to reflect the new State Capital Outlay allocation.

In October, the following revisions were made:

Changes were made to five District Services projects to support the West Valley Baseball Complex Renovation project. Funding from these projects will be used to supplement the bid award of all the bid packages as follows:

DS-06 Utility & Electrical Upgrades funding: $ 500,000

DS-07 Underground Water & Sewer Upgrades funding: $ 500,000

DS-12 Parking Lots, Roads & Sidewalk Replacement funding: $ 500,000

DS-13 Storm Water Control Upgrades funding: $ 250,000

DS-14 Security, Safety & Monitoring Upgrades funding: $ 250,000

Total: $2,000,000

WV-4 Outdoor Physical Education Facilities Upgrades. The budget for this project will be increased by $7,425,000 to cover awarding all bid packages, a change order contingency, furniture and equipment, and an audio/video system. Funding for this increase is derived from a combination of District Capital Outlay funds and Measure W funds as follows:

DS-06 Utility & Electrical Upgrades funding: $ 500,000

DS-07 Underground Water & Sewer Upgrades funding: $ 500,000

DS-10 Administration Building: $2,000,000

DS-12 Parking Lots, Roads & Sidewalk Repl. funding: $ 500,000

DS-13 Storm Water Control Upgrades funding: $ 250,000

DS-14 Security, Safety & Monitoring Upgrades funding: $ 250,000

WV-01 West Valley Program Contingency funding: $3,425,000

Total: $7,425,000

Mr. Castruita and Ms. Ryan reviewed the Measure C and Measure W financials. DS-04 – Police Building and Yard at West Valley College was highlighted during the review of the Measure W financials.

5. Closing Items

5.1 Staff Member Comments

None.

5.2 Committee Member Comments

None.

5.3 Adjournment

Meeting adjourned at 6:46pm.
